title: Production IAM & Operator Control Completion
decision_rationale: GL-214 implements narrow production IAM/operator-control hardening by constraining route-created operator roles to the current route-guard vocabulary and adding durable tenant-admin audit-chain events for operator create/revoke actions. It preserves Developer Preview and Controlled Preview boundaries and does not claim production SaaS, real-data, official SDK/package, live PostgreSQL production, compliance, or enterprise readiness.

current_iam_operator_state_summary.admin_token_validation: Admin token validation uses Bearer tokens and constant-time comparison; protected admin routes fail closed for missing or invalid credentials.
current_iam_operator_state_summary.operator_token_validation: Operator tokens use PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 storage with a deterministic lookup hash used only for narrowing before verification.
current_iam_operator_state_summary.revoked_inactive_handling: Revoked and inactive operators are excluded from authentication queries.
current_iam_operator_state_summary.tenant_context: Operator tenant context is server-derived from the stored operator row; request headers cannot override it.
current_iam_operator_state_summary.route_protection: Admin/operator control-plane routes are admin-token protected; normal routes use operator role guards when operator model is enabled.
current_iam_operator_state_summary.production_status: Baseline exists but production IAM is not complete.

production_iam_gap_assessment.admin_token: Single static admin token remains a P0 production IAM gap.
production_iam_gap_assessment.operator_tokens: Operator token storage and active/expiry checks exist, but automated lifecycle and external secret storage remain incomplete.
production_iam_gap_assessment.operator_roles: Routes recognize owner, grant_admin, and auditor; GL-214 aligns new route-created operators to that vocabulary.
production_iam_gap_assessment.audit: GL-214 adds durable audit-chain events for operator create/revoke actions.
production_iam_gap_assessment.identity_provider: No OIDC, SAML, SSO, MFA, browser login, or enterprise IAM provider is implemented.

implemented_hardening_summary: POST /admin/operators now rejects roles outside owner, grant_admin, and auditor.
POST /admin/operators appends a durable tenant_admin audit-chain event for operator_created without token material.
POST /admin/operators/{id}/revoke appends a durable tenant_admin audit-chain event for operator_revoked without token material.

admin_token_behavior.missing: Fails closed with safe admin_token_required response.
admin_token_behavior.invalid: Fails closed with safe admin_token_invalid response.
admin_token_behavior.leakage: Responses and audit events do not include raw admin token or authorization header.

operator_token_behavior.storage: PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 with deterministic lookup hash for narrowing.
operator_token_behavior.admin_routes: Operator tokens cannot create, list, get, or revoke operators through admin-only routes.
operator_token_behavior.normal_routes: Operator tokens authenticate normal operator-protected routes according to role.
operator_token_behavior.leakage: Safe responses do not include token_hash, token_lookup_hash, lookup_hash, or raw token except one-time create token.

revoked_inactive_operator_behavior.revoked: Revoked operators are set inactive and cannot authenticate.
revoked_inactive_operator_behavior.inactive: Inactive operators are excluded from authentication queries.
revoked_inactive_operator_behavior.expired: Expired and malformed expiry values fail closed.

operator_tenant_role_scope_boundary.tenant: Tenant ID is assigned at operator creation and server-derived during authentication.
operator_tenant_role_scope_boundary.role: Route-created roles are constrained to owner, grant_admin, and auditor.
operator_tenant_role_scope_boundary.scope: Operator lifecycle audit events use tenant_admin scope.
operator_tenant_role_scope_boundary.remaining_gap: This is not a full production RBAC or workspace policy engine.

audit_coverage_for_iam_operator_actions.operator_created: Durable audit-chain event added with safe operator resource and tenant_admin scope.
audit_coverage_for_iam_operator_actions.operator_revoked: Durable audit-chain event added with safe operator resource and tenant_admin scope.
audit_coverage_for_iam_operator_actions.secret_safety: Audit events do not contain raw tokens, token hashes, authorization headers, DSNs, private keys, or credentials.

production_readiness_impact.summary: Materially reduces the P0 Production IAM/operator-control blocker by adding durable lifecycle audit coverage and constraining new operator roles.

controlled_preview_impact.summary: Improves internal operator accountability for controlled preview use.

remaining_iam_blockers: Multi-admin governance and dual-control approval are not implemented.
Production secret lifecycle automation is incomplete.
Full production RBAC/policy administration is not implemented.
External SIEM/SOC/alerting integration is not implemented.
Production IAM break-glass procedures remain incomplete.

risk_register.risk: Operator lifecycle audit coverage was incomplete.
Route-created operator roles were not constrained to current route guards.

findings: Admin token behavior remains fail-closed for protected admin routes.
Operator authentication rejects revoked, inactive, expired, malformed, and unknown tokens.
GL-214 adds durable tenant-admin audit-chain coverage for operator lifecycle actions.
Production IAM remains blocked on broader identity, secret lifecycle, RBAC, workspace, and operations controls.
